claims.excerpt:
Supply: 1,849 MW

Total Supply is the sum of four supply categories: Live, Under Construction, Committed, and Early Stage.

claims.claim: An amended decree bans new hyperscale data centres (<10 hectares/70+MW) in the Netherlands except in Het Hogeland and Hollands Kroon
claims.excerpt: an amended decree has meant that no new hyperscale data centres (<10 hectares/70+ MW) are allowed to be constructed in the Netherlands, except for a few locations in the municipalities of Het Hogeland (e.g. Eemshaven) and Hollands Kroon (e.g. Agriport)

claims.claim: Amsterdam's draft umbrella zoning plan proposes a maximum connection capacity of 350 MVA within the municipality until 2030
claims.excerpt: a recent draft umbrella zoning plan suggesting that 350 MVA is the new maximum connection capacity within the municipality until 2030

claims.claim: Power distribution firms report no power available for data centres until 2030
claims.excerpt: many power distributions firms saying that they have no power available until 2030, thus limiting development

claims.excerpt: Between 2021 and 2023, the market expanded from 1,489.0MW to 1,638.0MW, reflecting a CAGR of 4.9% (Dutch Data Center Association, 2024) (Dutch Data Center Association, 2023).

claims.claim: The digital infrastructure industry contributes approximately €25.0 billion to the Dutch economy
claims.excerpt: the digital infrastructure industry contributes approximately €25.0 Bn ($26.9 Bn) to the national economy

claims.claim: A moratorium on Amsterdam data center development began in 2019 and was lifted in 2020
claims.excerpt: Major growth was seen between 2010 and 2018 and was then curtailed by a moratorium in 2019. Since the moratorium was lifted in 2020, development has picked up but the rate of activity has reduced

claims.claim: Pure DC signed a lease for a 78MW hyperscale campus in Westpoort, Amsterdam, securing 100MVA of power via a private substation

claims.excerpt: A hyperscale customer is leasing the entire 78MW campus, situated in Westpoort, Amsterdam...secured planning permissions, and 100MVA of power via a private substation.

claims.claim: Netherlands data center portfolio report covers 147 facilities (129 existing, 18 upcoming) across 47 regions
claims.sourceTitle: Netherlands Data Centers Portfolio: Existing & Upcoming Data Centers in Netherlands
claims.excerpt:
Discover Insights on 147 Data Center Facilities Across 47 Key Regions in the Netherlands...129 Existing Data Centers

18 Upcoming Data Centers

tensions.text: CleanBridge reports the Dutch data center market at 1,638.0 MW in 2023, roughly 1.7x higher than the DDA's 924 MW colocation figure (2024) and the Statista 951 MW figure used to derive geo.NL's 0.28 ratio — likely reflecting a broader 'total data center' scope (including enterprise/non-colo) versus the colocation-only scope the node relies on, but the gap is unreconciled.

Cushman & Wakefield's Amsterdam operational capacity of 852 MW is inconsistent with DDA's implied Amsterdam-region figure (≈70% of 924 MW national colocation ≈ 647 MW), suggesting either scope differences (all DC types vs colocation-only) or source disagreement within the same region feeding the node's cross-check.

DC Byte's Amsterdam 'Total Supply' of 1,849 MW includes Live+Under Construction+Committed+Early Stage capacity, far exceeding the ~852 MW operational figure and the national 924-951 MW colocation baselines the node's derivation relies on — a pipeline-vs-operational mismatch that could distort the ratio if pipeline figures are inadvertently used.

Regulatory caps (no new hyperscale <10ha/70MW+ sites outside Het Hogeland/Hollands Kroon; Amsterdam's proposed 350 MVA connection ceiling until 2030; power unavailable until 2030) constrain NL capacity growth independent of demand, which could invalidate any assumption that NL's share of CE-7 capacity (currently 0.28) will scale proportionally with regional AI/cloud demand growth.

absences: No explicit CE-7 (or other seven-country) total MW breakdown was found in these results to independently verify the 3,451 MW denominator used in the node's apportionment.
No source explained or reconciled the small but persistent discrepancy between Statista's 951 MW (2025) and DDA's 924 MW (2024) figures for Dutch colocation capacity.

No MW-level breakdown for secondary Dutch regions (Groningen, South Holland, North Brabant, Rotterdam, Eindhoven) was found, despite these being repeatedly cited as growth areas — only the ~70% Amsterdam Metropolitan Area share is quantified.
No updated 2025/2026 national colocation capacity figure (beyond the 2024 924 MW DDA figure) was found to corroborate Statista's 2025 951 MW estimate.

No quantification of total available or contracted grid capacity (in MW/MVA) at a national level was found, despite repeated qualitative references to grid congestion and power unavailability limiting growth.
